Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

uhttpd: increase CGI workers from 2 to 20, connections from 50 to 100

-n 2 allowed only 2 simultaneous CGI instances. With json-heartbeat.cgi
(and potentially other SSE streams) permanently holding slots, regular
CGI requests like pan/tilt motor commands had no available worker and
would queue indefinitely, appearing as 'pending' in the browser.

Increase -n from 2 to 20 to accommodate persistent SSE connections
(heartbeat, motor-stream, timegraph) plus concurrent API requests.
Increase -N from 50 to 100 to match the higher concurrency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: security champion tooltip background not rendering (#729)

The tooltip on the security champion card (showing which components a
user is champion for) had a transparent background, making it unreadable.

Three issues were fixed:

1. Styling approach: The CustomTooltip used styled() with
   slotProps.tooltip.className, but MUI Tooltip renders its popup in a
   portal so the styles never reached the popup element. Rewrote to use
   slotProps.tooltip.sx which directly targets the popup.

2. Non-existent CSS variable: The background used var(--bui-bg-surface-1)
   which does not exist in the BUI theme, resolving to transparent.

3. Hardcoded border color: Replaced 'gray' with var(--bui-border-1) for
   proper dark mode support.

Now uses var(--bui-bg-app) for background and var(--bui-border-1) for
border, both theme-aware with proper light/dark mode values.

commit.message:
fix(apple-silicon): fix macOS ARM64 build failure — half crate not linked (#3323)

## Problem

The macOS ARM64 CI build fails with:
```
error[E0432]: unresolved import `half`
  --> crates/bitnet-kernels/src/cpu/neon_mixed_precision.rs:14:5
```

## Root Cause

`bitnet-inference`'s `cpu` feature only enabled
`bitnet-kernels/cpu-optimized`
(SIMD targets: avx512, avx2, neon) but NOT `bitnet-kernels/cpu` (which
provides
`dep:half`). On aarch64, the `neon_mixed_precision` module requires
`half::f16`
but the crate wasn't linked.

Similarly, the root workspace `cpu` feature was missing
`bitnet-kernels/cpu`.

## Fixes

1. **Feature chain**: Add `bitnet-kernels/cpu` to both
`bitnet-inference/cpu` and
   root workspace `cpu` features
2. **Defense in depth**: Gate `neon_mixed_precision` module on `feature
= "cpu"`
   in addition to `target_arch = "aarch64"`
3. **Clippy**: Fix `needless_range_loop` in dense_forward attention
weights

## Testing

```bash
cargo check -p bitnet-inference --no-default-features --features cpu  # ✅
cargo clippy -p bitnet-kernels -p bitnet-inference --no-default-features --features cpu -- -D warnings  # ✅
```

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve DigitalOcean job progress tracking based on elapsed time

Calculate progress as elapsed time vs MaxRuntimeMinutes instead of
returning hardcoded 50% when running. Track job start times in a
dictionary, cap progress at 90% (100% only on completion), and fall
back to droplet created_at with 30min estimate when start time is
not tracked. Clean up tracking data on job completion/failure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: FTS5 corruption + local model crash

Switch FTS5 from external-content to regular mode (rowid-based triggers).
The old value-matching 'delete' triggers were unreliable in modernc.org/sqlite,
causing all write operations (UPDATE, DELETE, tag add/remove) to fail with
SQLITE_CORRUPT_VTAB (267). Regular FTS5 uses simple DELETE FROM fts WHERE
rowid = old.rowid — no value matching needed.

Added schema versioning (schema_version table) with auto-migration from v1
(external-content) to v2 (regular FTS5). Migration drops old FTS + triggers,
recreates as regular FTS5, and repopulates from history_entries.

Fixed local model crash by capping NumThreads to min(4, NumCPU) per sherpa-onnx
docs (was runtime.NumCPU() which is too aggressive for large models). Fixed
Language parameter: mapped 'auto' to empty string since sherpa-onnx expects
ISO codes or '' for auto-detection.

Added debug breadcrumbs before/after each sherpa-onnx call for crash diagnosis.
Removed execWithFTSRepair (no longer needed with regular FTS5).

commit.message:
perf: eliminate all LINQ from production code

Replace every LINQ extension method call across 79 production files with
imperative loops, List.Sort(), Array.Sort(), manual dictionary building,
foreach+break for FirstOrDefault, bool flags for Any/All, and HashSet
for Distinct.

Key files by call count replaced:
- RouteHandlers.cs: ~206 calls (request handling hot path)
- DataScaffold.cs: ~81 calls (startup metadata)
- RouteRegistrationExtensions.cs: ~63 calls (startup routes)
- ComputedFieldService.cs: ~29 calls (read/write path)
- ProxyRouteHandler.cs: ~29 calls (per-request proxy)
- ReportExecutor.cs: ~28 calls (report queries)
- WalDataProvider.cs: ~27 calls (WAL queries)
- SearchIndexing.cs: ~26 calls (full-text search)
- VectorIndex.cs: ~25 calls (similarity search)
- Plus 70 more files across Host, Data, Rendering, Runtime,
  Core, AI, CLI, and DataBrowser projects

All using System.Linq; directives removed. The only remaining
System.Linq reference is System.Linq.Expressions (expression trees)
in PropertyAccessorFactory.cs and ReportQuery.cs, which is not
query LINQ.

ImplicitUsings still adds global using System.Linq; automatically
but no LINQ methods are called anywhere in production code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(config): add missing locks to load_config and load_adapter_name_cache

Post-review fixes for two thread-safety gaps:

1. load_config() read CONFIG_FILE without config_lock, risking a corrupt
   read during a concurrent atomic write (save_device_volume, etc.).
   Now holds config_lock during the open+json.load.

2. load_adapter_name_cache() mutated _adapter_name_cache without holding
   _adapter_cache_lock. Direct callers (routes/api.py config save handler)
   could race with get_adapter_name() readers seeing partial data.
   Now holds _adapter_cache_lock for the entire reload.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: GitHub Actions AI maintainer workflow

Replaces the non-functional placeholder workflow with a real agentic
implementation that processes 'ai-task' issues automatically.

.github/workflows/ai-maintainer.yml
- Triggers on issues: [labeled] (not a cron schedule)
- Filters for label == 'ai-task' in the job condition
- Supports workflow_dispatch for manual re-processing
- Concurrency group prevents parallel runs on the same issue
- Installs zbx + openai, then runs ai_maintainer.py

.github/scripts/ai_maintainer.py
- Reads issue context from env vars (injected by the workflow)
- Calls GitHub Models API (gpt-4o, OpenAI-compatible via GITHUB_TOKEN)
  at https://models.inference.ai.azure.com
- Agentic loop (max 30 rounds) with 5 tools:
    read_file     — read any repo file
    list_files    — list a directory
    write_file    — create or overwrite a file
    validate      — run zbx validate <path> for YAML correctness
    finish        — signal completion with branch/PR title/body
- After finish(): creates branch, commits, pushes, opens PR via gh CLI
- Posts a comment on the issue with the PR link (or skip reason)
- Skips unclear/risky issues gracefully (SKIP: prefix convention)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

v3.0.2 (#576)

* feat(course-card): add success toasts for course enable/disable

Show a sonner success toast immediately after the user confirms:
  - Disable: "CS101 disabled" with the selected reason as description.
  - Enable:  "CS101 enabled".

Fires optimistically on confirm-click (the mutation uses optimistic

muted tint. tailwind-merge resolves the conflicting bg/border classes in
